only PNG and JPG will be supported

For JPG, just make sure it has no metadata, and again, the byte array is just the file contents

The idea is to use `.pio\libdeps\theflybringuptest\M5GFX\src\lgfx\utility\lgfx_pngle.c` and `.pio\libdeps\theflybringuptest\M5GFX\src\lgfx\utility\lgfx_tjpgd.c` directly without using a file-system, and also have constants to know how to make the sprites in-memory
Each array is a uint8_t array, with attributes like `const` so it lives in flash memory, maybe add `PROGMEM`?
The name of the array starts with `sprit_` and ends with whatever the file name is, without extension, and all illegal characters transformed to `_`, and do not allow consecutive `_` or trailing `_`.
If the file name ends with ".fw.png", ignore the `fw`.

agent_reply: Implemented the new compressed-byte-array converter in [tools/graphics_converter.py](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/tools/graphics_converter.py).

It now only accepts PNG/JPG/JPEG, strips metadata, recompresses PNG losslessly, emits `const uint8_t ...[] PROGMEM` arrays in [src/sprites.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/sprites.cpp), and writes externs plus `*_WIDTH`, `*_HEIGHT`, and `*_BYTES` macros in [inc/sprites.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/sprites.h).
That is a much healthier firmware asset than the raw RGB565 array. `pio run -e theflybringuptest` passes.

user_prompt: `lib\FlyGui` has a feature where a PNG file is loaded into a buffer, and this buffer can be reused in multiple views and items.
I need to remove this feature, the system will now draw by decoding on demand.
I have marked the section in `lib\FlyGui\readme.md` as struck-out
Remove this function from the actual code
The actual implementation will now use `lib\SpriteDraw\SpriteDraw.cpp` and maybe `lib\SpriteDraw\IconLookup.cpp` will be involved. `LittleFS` and actual file function will not be used.
agent_reply: I removed the RAM-loaded/reused PNG buffer path from FlyGui:
- Removed the LittleFS/SdFat image loading helpers from [FlyGui.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp).
The new draw path decodes on demand:
- It gets a compiled sprite via `IconLookup::getSprite()`.
I also removed the remaining LittleFS PNG draw from [SplashView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp), so splash now uses the compiled `sprit_splash` asset and `SpriteDraw`.
- `git diff --check` clean for the touched files.

user_prompt: hmmmm I'm getting a watchdog expiry crash in `loopTask_core0`, reset that task watchdog at the bottom of the loop
agent_reply: Added the watchdog reset at the bottom of `loopTask_core0()` in [src/main.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp), after `taskYIELD()`:
I also restored the missing `sprite_desc_t` definition in [inc/sprites.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/sprites.h), because the build was blocked by `IconLookup.h` needing that type.
Build passed. If the watchdog still trips, that means `AudioManager::pump_task()` or `Hotel::pollCore0()` is blocking long enough that execution never reaches the reset call.

user_prompt: for the `ErrorView` I want a change
I want to place a button with `sprite_doorreturn_50` for non fatal errors, clicking it or `BtnC` is now what dismisses the dialog. Remove the bottom message.

For fatal errors, place a button with `sprite_firstaidwifi_50` and when clicking it, it does a best effort at launching the default secure soft AP and then showing the `WifiApModeView` . The polling loop for this will need to have `WifiManager` polling, and maybe `BattTracker::poll();` too.

Make sure that the launching of the default secure softAP does not require the successful loading of NVS.
